message:
feat(abi): port NUMA memory-policy family (set/get_mempolicy + mbind + migrate_pages + move_pages + set_mempolicy_home_node) (bd-i8ind)

Six NUMA libc syscall wrappers, each forwarding via libc::syscall and
bridging errno through a new raw_syscall_with_errno_long helper that
mirrors the existing raw_syscall_with_errno but preserves the long
return needed by these calls (status, returned page count, etc.):

  - set_mempolicy(mode, *nodemask, maxnode) (SYS_set_mempolicy=238) —
    set the calling thread's default NUMA policy.
  - get_mempolicy(*mode, *nodemask, maxnode, addr, flags)
    (SYS_get_mempolicy=239) — read the policy for the thread or for a
    specific address.
  - mbind(addr, len, mode, *nodemask, maxnode, flags)
    (SYS_mbind=237) — set NUMA policy for a virtual memory range.
  - migrate_pages(pid, maxnode, *old_nodes, *new_nodes)
    (SYS_migrate_pages=256) — move all pages of pid (or 0 = self) on
    old_nodes to new_nodes.
  - move_pages(pid, count, **pages, *nodes, *status, flags)
    (SYS_move_pages=279) — move listed pages or query their current
    NUMA nodes when nodes is NULL.
  - set_mempolicy_home_node(start, len, home_node, flags) (Linux 5.17+,
    SYS_set_mempolicy_home_node=450) — set the preferred NUMA home
    node for an existing memory range.

Tests: 6 abi tests cover get_mempolicy default for calling thread,
set+get round-trip with MPOL_DEFAULT, mbind invalid-mode rejection
(EINVAL), migrate_pages ESRCH for a nonexistent pid, move_pages
query-only on a self-allocated and touched page, and
set_mempolicy_home_node either-succeeds-or-returns-known-errno on the
documented (0,0,0,0) no-op shape. All accept ENOSYS / EPERM as
sandbox/no-NUMA fallbacks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-4l5om","title":"PORT: __wait3/4 + __waitid + __dup3 + __pipe2 + __getcwd + __getlogin + __getlogin_r + __fcntl64 + __clock_settime + __clock_nanosleep (glibc reserved aliases)","description":"Port the remaining glibc reserved-namespace aliases for already-shipped public functions: __wait3, __wait4, __waitid (process_abi); __dup3, __pipe2 (io_abi); __getcwd, __getlogin, __getlogin_r, __fcntl64 (unistd_abi); __clock_settime, __clock_nanosleep (time_abi). glibc emits these as both internal-use entry points and as the symbols libstdc++ / NSS modules / libdl / libpthread internals link against. Same delegating-alias pattern as the previously-shipped __pthread_*, __libc_dlopen_mode, __strchrnul / __memrchr / __getline alias families. Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust extern "C" wrappers that delegate to the public names. Tests verify each alias resolves and produces the same observable behavior as its public counterpart on a representative input.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T12:38:25.191527108Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T12:47:48.406262394Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T12:47:48.404202193Z","close_reason":"shipped: 10 glibc reserved-namespace aliases (__wait3/4 + __waitid + __dup3/pipe2 + __getcwd/login/login_r + __clock_settime/nanosleep) delegating to existing public-name impls + 10 abi tests across 4 test files, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-4ny4d","title":"PORT: mseal + memfd_secret + rseq + cachestat (modern Linux syscall wrappers)","description":"Add four modern Linux syscall wrappers. (1) mseal(addr, len, flags) -> int (Linux 6.10+, glibc 2.40, SYS_mseal=462) — seal a memory region against future remap/protect/munmap. (2) memfd_secret(flags) -> int (Linux 5.14+, SYS_memfd_secret=447) — create an anonymous file backed by memory inaccessible from /proc, ptrace, etc. (3) rseq(*rseq, len, flags, sig) -> int (Linux 4.18+, SYS_rseq=334) — register a restartable sequences area for per-CPU concurrency. (4) cachestat(fd, *cstat_range, *cstat, flags) -> int (Linux 6.5+, syscall 451) — query page cache statistics for a file range. All four forward via libc::syscall with errno bridging via set_abi_errno; opaque pointers for the structs since their layouts are kernel-version-specific. NULL-pointer guards where applicable.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T16:55:37.463706734Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T16:55:37.463706734Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

(4) cachestat(fd, *cstat_range, *cstat, flags) -> int (Linux 6.5+, syscall 451) — query page cache statistics for a file range. All four forward via libc::syscall with errno bridging via set_abi_errno; opaque pointers for the structs since their layouts are kernel-version-specific. @@ -836,6 +836,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-i3om4","title":"[HIGH] runtime_policy::ffi_pcc tests leak global state across parallel test threads","description":"## Symptom

`cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --lib` fails with:

```
thread 'runtime_policy::tests::ffi_pcc_decide_uses_certificate_gate_for_memcpy' panicked at crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/runtime_policy.rs:2168
test result: FAILED. 170 passed; 1 failed
```

Running the same test in isolation (`cargo test ... ffi_pcc_decide_uses_certificate_gate_for_memcpy -- --nocapture`) passes cleanly: '1 passed; 0 failed; 170 filtered out'.

## Diagnosis

The test already acquires `ffi_pcc_lock()` and calls `reset_ffi_pcc_state_for_tests`, `reset_decision_contract_machine_for_tests`, `enable_runtime_kernel_for_tests`, `set_mode_state_for_tests(MODE_HARDENED)`. That pattern suggests the author was aware of test-global-state coupling. The fact that the assertion at line 2168 (`assert_eq!(decision.policy_id, FFI_PCC_POLICY_BASE + 10)`) fires only under concurrent test scheduling means **at least one of those resets does not cover all mutable state that another test modifies concurrently** — either a static/lazy cache, a thread-local, or a singleton that is not inside the lock's scope.

Adjacent tests in the same module (`ffi_pcc_decide_uses_certificate_gate_for_snprintf` et al.) use the same lock and reset helpers and presumably race on the same shared state.

## Fix direction

1. Grep for every `static ATOMIC` / `LazyLock` / `thread_local` / `Mutex<` in runtime_policy.rs that the certificate gate path touches. Classify which are covered by `ffi_pcc_lock` vs `decision_contract_machine` vs neither.
2. Any state touched by `decide()` along the `lookup_active_ffi_pcc_certificate` -> `ffi_pcc_decision` path that is not inside one of the reset-helpers is a candidate leak.
3. Confirmed repro: run the full `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --lib` twice in a row; the failure is sometimes the 'memcpy' test, sometimes 'snprintf' or another adjacent one — classic test-ordering-dependent leak.

## Blast radius

Blocks `cargo test --workspace`. Part of the same full-review batch as bd-g2bd5 (glibc_internal_abi_test heap corruption). Caught by cc-libc FULL REVIEW MODE.

## Verification

- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --lib runtime_policy::tests::ffi_pcc_decide_uses_certificate_gate_for_memcpy -- --nocapture` passes (1/1).
- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --lib` fails at the same test (170/171).

+{"id":"bd-i8ind","title":"PORT: NUMA memory-policy family (set/get_mempolicy + mbind + migrate_pages + move_pages + set_mempolicy_home_node)","description":"Add the NUMA memory-policy family of Linux libc syscall wrappers. (1) set_mempolicy(mode, *nodemask, maxnode) -> long (SYS_set_mempolicy=238) sets the calling threads default NUMA policy. (2) get_mempolicy(*mode, *nodemask, maxnode, addr, flags) -> long (SYS_get_mempolicy=239) reads the policy for the calling thread or for a specific address. (3) mbind(addr, len, mode, *nodemask, maxnode, flags) -> long (SYS_mbind=237) binds a memory range to a NUMA policy. (4) migrate_pages(pid, maxnode, *old_nodes, *new_nodes) -> long (SYS_migrate_pages=256) migrates pages in another process between NUMA nodes. (5) move_pages(pid, count, **pages, *nodes, *status, flags) -> long (SYS_move_pages=279) moves the listed pages to specified target nodes. (6) set_mempolicy_home_node(start, len, home_node, flags) -> long (Linux 5.17+, SYS_set_mempolicy_home_node=450) sets the preferred NUMA home node for a memory range. All forward via libc::syscall; raw_syscall_with_errno bridges errno; returns are -1 on failure.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T17:04:42.603609331Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T17:04:42.603609331Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ibh7","title":"Divergent ABI source scanning between build.rs and check_replacement_guard.sh breaks standalone build","description":"`crates/frankenlibc-abi/build.rs` scans all `.rs` files in `src/` for `libc::` callthroughs, which catches `host_resolve.rs:51 libc::dlvsym` and breaks the `standalone` build. However, `scripts/check_replacement_guard.sh` only scans `*_abi.rs` files, missing `host_resolve.rs` entirely. Furthermore, `build.rs` does not implement the newly added `source_scan_exceptions` from `replacement_profile.json`.\n\nTo fix:\n1. Make `check_replacement_guard.sh` scan all `.rs` files (or align the scopes).\n2. Update `build.rs` to respect `source_scan_exceptions` so `host_resolve::dlvsym` (and any future exceptions) can be whitelisted.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-18T19:12:58.484175575Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T20:33:37.318380234Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T20:33:37.316445662Z","close_reason":"No repro; build.rs and replacement guard already align on *_abi.rs scanning and source_scan_exceptions","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -5047,6 +5047,198 @@ pub unsafe extern "C" fn cachestat(
     unsafe { raw_syscall_with_errno(rc) }
 }
 
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+// NUMA memory policy (set/get_mempolicy + mbind + migrate_pages + move_pages
+// + set_mempolicy_home_node)
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+#[inline]
+unsafe fn raw_syscall_with_errno_long(rc: libc::c_long) -> libc::c_long {
+    if rc < 0 {
+        let e = unsafe { *libc::__errno_location() };
+        unsafe { set_abi_errno(e) };
+        return -1;
+    }
+    rc
+}
+
+/// Linux `set_mempolicy(mode, *nodemask, maxnode) -> long`
+/// (`SYS_set_mempolicy = 238`) — set the calling thread's default
+/// NUMA memory policy.
+///
+/// # Safety
+///
+/// `nodemask`, when non-NULL, must point to at least
+/// `(maxnode + 7) / 8` readable bytes describing a valid node bitmap.
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn set_mempolicy(
+    mode: c_int,
+    nodemask: *const c_ulong,
+    maxnode: c_ulong,
+) -> c_long {
+    // SAFETY: forwarding to the kernel.
+    let rc = unsafe {
+        libc::syscall(
+            libc::SYS_set_mempolicy,
+            mode as libc::c_long,
+            nodemask as libc::c_long,
+            maxnode as libc::c_long,
+        )
+    };
+    unsafe { raw_syscall_with_errno_long(rc) }
+}
+
+/// Linux `get_mempolicy(*mode, *nodemask, maxnode, addr, flags) ->
+/// long` (`SYS_get_mempolicy = 239`) — read the NUMA policy for the
+/// calling thread or for a specific address.
+///
+/// # Safety
+///
+/// `mode`, when non-NULL, must point to writable `c_int` storage.
+/// `nodemask`, when non-NULL, must point to at least
+/// `(maxnode + 7) / 8` writable bytes.
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn get_mempolicy(
+    mode: *mut c_int,
+    nodemask: *mut c_ulong,
+    maxnode: c_ulong,
+    addr: *mut c_void,
+    flags: c_ulong,
+) -> c_long {
+    // SAFETY: forwarding to the kernel.
+    let rc = unsafe {
+        libc::syscall(
+            libc::SYS_get_mempolicy,
+            mode as libc::c_long,
+            nodemask as libc::c_long,
+            maxnode as libc::c_long,
+            addr as libc::c_long,
+            flags as libc::c_long,
+        )
+    };
+    unsafe { raw_syscall_with_errno_long(rc) }
+}
+
+/// Linux `mbind(addr, len, mode, *nodemask, maxnode, flags) -> long`
+/// (`SYS_mbind = 237`) — set the NUMA memory policy for a range of
+/// virtual memory.
+///
+/// # Safety
+///
+/// `addr` must point to a memory region of at least `len` bytes.
+/// `nodemask`, when non-NULL, follows the same shape as in
+/// [`set_mempolicy`].
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn mbind(
+    addr: *mut c_void,
+    len: c_ulong,
+    mode: c_int,
+    nodemask: *const c_ulong,
+    maxnode: c_ulong,
+    flags: c_uint,
+) -> c_long {
+    // SAFETY: forwarding to the kernel.
+    let rc = unsafe {
+        libc::syscall(
+            libc::SYS_mbind,
+            addr as libc::c_long,
+            len as libc::c_long,
+            mode as libc::c_long,
+            nodemask as libc::c_long,
+            maxnode as libc::c_long,
+            flags as libc::c_long,
+        )
+    };
+    unsafe { raw_syscall_with_errno_long(rc) }
+}
+
+/// Linux `migrate_pages(pid, maxnode, *old_nodes, *new_nodes) ->
+/// long` (`SYS_migrate_pages = 256`) — move all pages of `pid`
+/// (or 0 for the calling process) on `old_nodes` to the
+/// corresponding entries in `new_nodes`.
+///
+/// # Safety
+///
+/// `old_nodes` and `new_nodes`, when non-NULL, must each point to at
+/// least `(maxnode + 7) / 8` readable bytes.
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn migrate_pages(
+    pid: c_int,
+    maxnode: c_ulong,
+    old_nodes: *const c_ulong,
+    new_nodes: *const c_ulong,
+) -> c_long {
+    // SAFETY: forwarding to the kernel.
+    let rc = unsafe {
+        libc::syscall(
+            libc::SYS_migrate_pages,
+            pid as libc::c_long,
+            maxnode as libc::c_long,
+            old_nodes as libc::c_long,
+            new_nodes as libc::c_long,
+        )
+    };
+    unsafe { raw_syscall_with_errno_long(rc) }
+}
+
+/// Linux `move_pages(pid, count, **pages, *nodes, *status, flags) ->
+/// long` (`SYS_move_pages = 279`) — move the listed pages (in
+/// process `pid`, or the calling process if 0) to the specified
+/// target NUMA nodes. If `nodes` is NULL the call only queries
+/// current locations into `status`.
+///
+/// # Safety
+///
+/// `pages` must point to an array of `count` pointers. `nodes`,
+/// when non-NULL, must point to `count` `c_int` entries. `status`,
+/// when non-NULL, must point to writable `count` `c_int` entries.
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn move_pages(
+    pid: c_int,
+    count: c_ulong,
+    pages: *const *mut c_void,
+    nodes: *const c_int,
+    status: *mut c_int,
+    flags: c_int,
+) -> c_long {
+    // SAFETY: forwarding to the kernel.
+    let rc = unsafe {
+        libc::syscall(
+            libc::SYS_move_pages,
+            pid as libc::c_long,
+            count as libc::c_long,
+            pages as libc::c_long,
+            nodes as libc::c_long,
+            status as libc::c_long,
+            flags as libc::c_long,
+        )
+    };
+    unsafe { raw_syscall_with_errno_long(rc) }
+}
+
+/// Linux `set_mempolicy_home_node(start, len, home_node, flags) ->
+/// long` (Linux 5.17+, `SYS_set_mempolicy_home_node = 450`) — set
+/// the preferred NUMA home node for an existing memory range.
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn set_mempolicy_home_node(
+    start: c_ulong,
+    len: c_ulong,
+    home_node: c_ulong,
+    flags: c_ulong,
+) -> c_long {
+    // SAFETY: forwarding to the kernel.
+    let rc = unsafe {
+        libc::syscall(
+            libc::SYS_set_mempolicy_home_node,
+            start as libc::c_long,
+            len as libc::c_long,
+            home_node as libc::c_long,
+            flags as libc::c_long,
+        )
+    };
+    unsafe { raw_syscall_with_errno_long(rc) }
+}
+
